title: RFC 9309 Matching, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Go Adapter · Execution Trace: Robotstxt Adapter
summary: The crawler trusts a Go package name, matches the first rule, fetches robots from the wrong service, ignores encoded paths, and treats an outage as permission. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go robotstxt adapter increment in conformance-tested robots decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Wrap temoto/robotstxt behind owned types and compare library behavior to RFC 9309 conformance fixtures and documented deviations.
Classify success, redirects, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, stale cache, and owner-stop states conservatively.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Robotstxt Adapter model · Theory Robotstxt Adapter, keep Robotstxt Adapter connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Wrap temoto/robotstxt behind owned types and compare library behavior to RFC 9309 conformance fixtures and documented deviations.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Robotstxt Adapter model · Theory Robotstxt Adapter, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Robotstxt Adapter precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Robotstxt Adapter model · Theory Robotstxt Adapter, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 8206 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RFC 9309 matching, response states, caching, Go package conformance, and extension restraint; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Wrap temoto/robotstxt behind owned types and compare library behavior to RFC 9309 conformance fixtures and documented deviations.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Robotstxt Adapter model · Theory Robotstxt Adapter, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Using a mature package removes need for protocol tests.
